name: Tacos Filled with Spicy Flaked Fish & Tomatil
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Juice from pickled jalapeno peppers
recipeInstructions:
From "Mexican Light Cooking," by Kathi Long (Perigee Books by Putnam).
1. Halve or quarter the fish filets so they will fit into a medium-sized saucepan.
2. Combine the chicken broth, jalapeno juice, and lime juice in the saucepan.
3. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-low.
4. Add the fish pieces and simmer 3 to 4 minutes.
5. Remove the pan from the heat and let the fish cool in the liquid for 30 minutes.
6. Remove the fish and pat dry with paper towels.
7. Break the fish into flakes using your fingers or a fork, then add the scallions and tomato.
8. Season with salt and pepper.
9. Arrange 2 tablespoons of the fish filling down the center of each taco shell.
10. Divide the lettuce among the shells and top each taco with 1 tablespoon tomatillo salsa.
PER SERVING: 295 calories, 28 g protein, 26 g carbohydrate, 10 g fat (2 g saturated), 44 mg cholesterol, 406 mg sodium, 3 g fiber.
